rbho_start_here() {
  # No sentinel — works pre-kindle (probes filesystem only)

  buc_doc_brief "Probe-aware menu — route learner into the handbook track that fits their state"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"

  # --- Preamble ---
  buh_section "Recipe Bottle — Onboarding Start"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "  " "Recipe Bottle" "${z_docs}" " builds container images with supply-chain provenance"
  buh_t   "  and runs untrusted containers behind enforced network isolation."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "  This menu points you at handbook tracks — self-describing teaching"
  buh_t   "  documents that explain concepts and show you live probe status."
  buh_e

  # --- Foundation ---
  buh_section "Foundation"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "    Configure your Repo's Environment"
  buh_tltlt "      Universal prerequisite. " "Tabtargets" "${z_docs}#Tabtarget" ", " "Regimes" "${z_docs}#Regime" ","
  buh_tltlt "      " "BURS" "${z_docs}#BURS" " setup, validation, " "Logs" "${z_docs}#Log" ". Local-only, no cloud."
  buh_tT  "        " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_CRASH_COURSE}"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "    Install " "Retriever" "${z_docs}#Retriever" " Credentials"
  buh_t   "      For joining an established project."
  buh_tlt "      Place your " "RBRA" "${z_docs}#RBRA" " credential file, verify, confirm you can pull images."
  buh_tT  "        " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_RETRIEVER}"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "    Install " "Director" "${z_docs}#Director" " Credentials"
  buh_t   "      For joining an established project."
  buh_tlt "      Place your " "RBRA" "${z_docs}#RBRA" " credential file, verify, confirm you can build and publish."
  buh_tT  "        " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_DIRECTOR}"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "    Start a " "Crucible" "${z_docs}#Crucible" " Using Local Builds"
  buh_tltlt "      The " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " " "Crucible" "${z_docs}#Crucible" " runs Claude Code in a container that can"
  buh_t   "      only reach Anthropic. Requires a Claude OAuth subscription."
  buh_t   "      Steps:"
  buh_tltltltlt "        * Build images locally      — " "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" "/" "Pentacle" "${z_docs}#Pentacle" " and " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" ""
  buh_tltlt   "        * Configure local network   — amend " "Nameplate" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" " " "RBRN" "${z_docs}#RBRN" " file"
  buh_tltlt   "        * Start the sandbox         — " "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" " the " "Crucible" "${z_docs}#Crucible" ""
  buh_tltlt   "        * Shell into the container  — " "Rack" "${z_docs}#Rack" " the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" ""
  buh_t   "      No cloud, no credentials beyond your own."
  buh_tT  "        " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_FIRST_CRUCIBLE}"
  buh_e

  # --- Create Payor and Depot ---
  buh_section "Create Payor and Depot"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "  A " "Depot" "${z_docs}#Depot" " is the facility where the team's container images are"
  buh_t   "  built and stored — the ground truth other tracks rest on."
  buh_e
  buh_tltltlt "    " "Payor" "${z_docs}#Payor" " — establish a " "Manor" "${z_docs}#Manor" " and provision the " "Depot" "${z_docs}#Depot" ""
  buh_tT  "        " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR_HB}"
  buh_e
  buh_tltltlt "    " "Governor" "${z_docs}#Governor" " — administer service accounts for " "Directors" "${z_docs}#Director" " and " "Retrievers" "${z_docs}#Retriever" ""
  buh_tT  "        " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_GOVERNOR_HB}"
  buh_e

  # --- Director subtracks ---
  buh_section "Director Subtracks"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "    Your First Cloud Build"
  buh_tlt "      Provision the builder toolchain, " "Ordain" "${z_docs}#Ordain" " your first"
  buh_tlt "      " "Vessel" "${z_docs}#Vessel" " via Cloud Build, and tour the result."
  buh_t   "      Steps:"
  buh_tlt "        * Inscribe the " "Reliquary" "${z_docs}#Reliquary" " — provision builder tool images"
  buh_tltlt "        * " "Conjure" "${z_docs}#Conjure" " " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " — first tethered cloud build"
  buh_tltltltlt "        * Tour: " "Tally" "${z_docs}#Tally" ", " "Vouch" "${z_docs}#Vouch" ", " "Plumb" "${z_docs}#Plumb" ", " "Pouch" "${z_docs}#Pouch" " — inspect images and SLSA"
  buh_tlt "        * " "Summon" "${z_docs}#Summon" " — pull the hallmark locally"
  buh_tltlt "        * " "Abjure" "${z_docs}#Abjure" " and " "Rekon" "${z_docs}#Rekon" " — hallmark lifecycle"
  buh_t   "      Requires: Director credentials and a provisioned Depot."
  buh_tT  "        " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_FIRST_BUILD}"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "    " "Airgap" "${z_docs}#Airgap" " Cloud Build"
  buh_tlt "      Build with zero upstream access. " "Enshrine" "${z_docs}#Enshrine" " mirrors base images"
  buh_t   "      into the Depot so Cloud Build never reaches the internet."
  buh_t   "      Steps:"
  buh_tlt "        * " "Enshrine" "${z_docs}#Enshrine" " base images — mirror upstream into the Depot"
  buh_tltlt "        * " "Conjure" "${z_docs}#Conjure" " " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " airgapped — same vessel, full isolation"
  buh_tlt "        * Compare " "Plumb" "${z_docs}#Plumb" " output — tethered vs airgap side by side"
  buh_t   "      Requires: Reliquary inscribed (previous track)."
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "    " "Bind" "${z_docs}#Bind" " — Safe PlantUML Container"
  buh_t   "      Mirror an upstream image by digest — no Dockerfile, no build."
  buh_t   "      PlantUML renders diagrams but its Docker Hub image could"
  buh_t   "      phone home. Bind pins it; the Sentry blocks all egress."
  buh_t   "      Steps:"
  buh_tltlt "        * " "Bind" "${z_docs}#Bind" " " "PlantUML" "${z_docs}#Bind" " — pin upstream image by digest"
  buh_tlt "        * Inspect " "Vouch" "${z_docs}#Vouch" " verdict — digest-pin, no SLSA (image not built here)"
  buh_tltlt "        * " "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" " the " "pluml" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" " Crucible — render a diagram, observe blocked egress"
  buh_t   "      You get the tool without the risk."
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "    " "Graft" "${z_docs}#Graft" " — Local Image Publishing"
  buh_t   "      Push a locally-built image to the Depot. The user owns the"
  buh_t   "      entire build — SLSA cannot vouch for this image. Vouch verdict"
  buh_t   "      is GRAFTED: an explicit signal that provenance stops at the"
  buh_t   "      local machine. Not the enterprise path for safe image creation."
  buh_t   "      Steps:"
  buh_tlt "        * " "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " a vessel image locally"
  buh_tlt "        * " "Graft" "${z_docs}#Graft" " — push local image to the Depot"
  buh_tlt "        * Inspect " "Vouch" "${z_docs}#Vouch" " verdict — GRAFTED, no provenance chain"
  buh_t   "      Development and prototyping workflow, not production supply chain."
  buh_e

}

######################################################################
# Configure your Repo's Environment — universal prerequisite handbook
#
# Hands-on walk-through: run commands, read output, configure your
# station. By the end, the learner has working local tooling and
# understands tabtargets, regimes, and the render/validate pattern.

rbho_crash_course() {
  # No sentinel — works pre-kindle (probes filesystem only)

  buc_doc_brief "Configure your Repo's Environment — tabtargets, regimes, station setup, logs"

content.content:
[1;37mStart a Crucible Using Local Builds[0m

A [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible]8;;\[0m is a sandboxed container environment with enforced
network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation
and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials
beyond your own Claude subscription.

This track uses the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo]8;;\[0m [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate]8;;\[0m: a Claude Code sandbox that can
only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked.

Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate
inside the container via copy/paste from your browser).

[1;37mStep 1 — Build images locally[0m

   A [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel]8;;\[0m is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile
   and build context. A [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m is a specific build instance,
   identified by a timestamp tag.

   [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge]8;;\[0m builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no
   registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running
   container without cloud image backup.

   [1;37mStep 1.1 — Kludge the sentry[0m

      The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry]8;;\[0m is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables
      and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist
      are reachable from inside.

      Kludge it:

         [36mtt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh[0m[36m rbev-sentry-deb-tether[0m

      The output prints a hallmark tag — something like
      [36mk260413155458-f9dcb6d9[0m
      Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2.

       [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found

      [1;33mKludge requires a clean git tree.[0m
      If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by
      design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse.

   [1;37mStep 1.2 — Kludge the bottle[0m

      The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m is your workload container — where Claude Code
      runs. The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo]8;;\[0m bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI.

      This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the
      bottle AND drives the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m into the nameplate
      automatically:

         [36mtt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh[0m[36m ccyolo[0m

      Two kludge commands, two different jobs:
        [36mrbw-fk[0m  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else.
        [36mrbw-cKB[0m — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark.

      The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone
      and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,
      so the tool does the driving for you.

      [36m [*] [0mKludge-tagged bottle image found locally

[1;37mStep 2 — Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate[0m

   A [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate]8;;\[0m is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies
   what [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m to use for [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry]8;;\[0m, [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pentacle\Pentacle]8;;\[0m, and
   [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m

   [36m   .rbk/ccyolo/rbrn.env[0m

   The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRN\RBRN]8;;\[0m file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry
   and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove
   its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand.

   Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1
   into the [36mRBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=[0m

   This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmarks]8;;\[0m are and
   where they live. You just saw the automated version with the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m;
   now you see the mechanism underneath.

    [*] [36mRBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK[0m = k260413155458-f9dcb6d9
    [*] [36mRBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK[0m = k260414083313-ce8b8648

   After editing, commit the change — the next [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge]8;;\[0m will need a
   clean tree:

   [36m   git add .rbk/ccyolo/rbrn.env[0m
   [36m   git commit -m "Drive sentry hallmark into ccyolo nameplate"[0m

[1;37mStep 3 — Charge the crucible[0m

   [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge]8;;\[0m starts three containers from your nameplate:

     [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry]8;;\[0m   — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist
     [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pentacle\Pentacle]8;;\[0m  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle
     [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)

   The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the
   network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules.

      [36mtt/rbw-cC.Charge.ccyolo.sh[0m

   Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local
   Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits
   for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then
   starts the bottle.

   [36m [*] [0mCrucible ccyolo is charged (bottle container running)

[1;37mStep 4 — Enter the container and run Claude Code[0m

   SSH into the bottle:

      [36mtt/rbw-cS.SshTo.ccyolo.sh[0m

   You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains
   a small sample project. Run Claude Code:

   [36m   claude[0m

   Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —
   copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude
   subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal.

   [1;33mThe ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version.[0m
   Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not
   work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec
   (github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you
   update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually.

   Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —
   no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,
   this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real
   security boundary, not the tool permission system.

   Try your first interaction:

   [36m   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?[0m

   Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code.
   The workspace persists across charge/quench cycles — your changes
   survive restarts.

   Why SSH instead of docker exec?

   Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's
   interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper
   login session with full terminal negotiation.

   If SSH fails, [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Rack\Rack]8;;\[0m is the diagnostic fallback —
   docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:

      [36mtt/rbw-cr.Rack.sh[0m[36m ccyolo[0m

[1;37mStep 5 — Verify network containment[0m

   From inside the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable.
   The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo]8;;\[0m nameplate allows Anthropic and example.com (a test
   target). Everything else is blocked.

   Run these curl commands inside the bottle:

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com[0m
      Expected: 404 (API wants auth, not bare GET)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://claude.ai[0m
      Expected: 403 (web app)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com[0m
      Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com[0m
      Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://registry.npmjs.org[0m
      Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)

   The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry]8;;\[0m enforces this with two layers:
   dnsmasq resolves only whitelisted domains; iptables drops
   packets to any IP not in the CIDR allowlist. Both layers
   must agree for traffic to pass.

   example.com is included in the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo]8;;\[0m [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate]8;;\[0m specifically
   for this verification step — it proves the allowlist works
   for a non-Anthropic domain.

[1;37mStep 6 — Quench the crucible[0m

   [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Quench\Quench]8;;\[0m stops and removes all three containers and the
   enclave network:

      [36mtt/rbw-cQ.Quench.ccyolo.sh[0m

   Clean shutdown. The images stay cached locally — your next charge
   reuses them instantly.

[1;37mThe iteration loop[0m

You now have the full local cycle:

  1. Edit the Dockerfile or entrypoint
  2. Commit your changes ([34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge]8;;\[0m needs a clean tree)
  3. [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge]8;;\[0m the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m:

[1;37mStart a Crucible Using Local Builds[0m

A [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible]8;;\[0m is a sandboxed container environment with enforced
network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation
and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials
beyond your own Claude subscription.

This track uses the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo]8;;\[0m [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate]8;;\[0m: a Claude Code sandbox that can
only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked.

Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate
inside the container via copy/paste from your browser).

[1;37mStep 1 — Build images locally[0m

   A [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel]8;;\[0m is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile
   and build context. A [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m is a specific build instance,
   identified by a timestamp tag.

   [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge]8;;\[0m builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no
   registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running
   container without cloud image backup.

   [1;37mStep 1.1 — Kludge the sentry[0m

      The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry]8;;\[0m is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables
      and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist
      are reachable from inside.

      Kludge it:

         [36mtt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh[0m[36m rbev-sentry-deb-tether[0m

      The output prints a hallmark tag — something like
      [36mk260413155458-f9dcb6d9[0m
      Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2.

       [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found

      [1;33mKludge requires a clean git tree.[0m
      If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by
      design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse.

   [1;37mStep 1.2 — Kludge the bottle[0m

      The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m is your workload container — where Claude Code
      runs. The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo]8;;\[0m bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI.

      This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the
      bottle AND drives the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m into the nameplate
      automatically:

         [36mtt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh[0m[36m ccyolo[0m

      Two kludge commands, two different jobs:
        [36mrbw-fk[0m  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else.
        [36mrbw-cKB[0m — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark.

      The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone
      and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,
      so the tool does the driving for you.

      [36m [*] [0mKludge-tagged bottle image found locally

[1;37mStep 2 — Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate[0m

   A [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate]8;;\[0m is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies
   what [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m to use for [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry]8;;\[0m, [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pentacle\Pentacle]8;;\[0m, and
   [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m

   [36m   .rbk/ccyolo/rbrn.env[0m

   The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRN\RBRN]8;;\[0m file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry
   and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove
   its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand.

   Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1
   into the [36mRBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=[0m

   This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmarks]8;;\[0m are and
   where they live. You just saw the automated version with the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m;
   now you see the mechanism underneath.

    [*] [36mRBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK[0m = k260413155458-f9dcb6d9
    [*] [36mRBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK[0m = k260414083313-ce8b8648

   After editing, commit the change — the next [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge]8;;\[0m will need a
   clean tree:

   [36m   git add .rbk/ccyolo/rbrn.env[0m
   [36m   git commit -m "Drive sentry hallmark into ccyolo nameplate"[0m

[1;37mStep 3 — Charge the crucible[0m

   [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge]8;;\[0m starts three containers from your nameplate:

     [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry]8;;\[0m   — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist
     [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pentacle\Pentacle]8;;\[0m  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle
     [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)

   The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the
   network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules.

      [36mtt/rbw-cC.Charge.ccyolo.sh[0m

   Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local
   Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits
   for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then
   starts the bottle.

   [36m [*] [0mCrucible ccyolo is charged (bottle container running)

[1;37mStep 4 — Enter the container and run Claude Code[0m

   SSH into the bottle:

      [36mtt/rbw-cS.SshTo.ccyolo.sh[0m

   You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains
   a small sample project. Run Claude Code:

   [36m   claude[0m

   Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —
   copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude
   subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal.

   [1;33mThe ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version.[0m
   Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not
   work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec
   (github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you
   update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually.

   Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —
   no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,
   this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real
   security boundary, not the tool permission system.

   Try your first interaction:

   [36m   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?[0m

   Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code.
   The workspace persists across charge/quench cycles — your changes
   survive restarts.

   Why SSH instead of docker exec?

   Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's
   interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper
   login session with full terminal negotiation.

   If SSH fails, [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Rack\Rack]8;;\[0m is the diagnostic fallback —
   docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:

      [36mtt/rbw-cr.Rack.sh[0m[36m ccyolo[0m

[1;37mStep 5 — Verify network containment[0m

   From inside the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable.
   The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo]8;;\[0m nameplate allows Anthropic and example.com (a test
   target). Everything else is blocked.

   Run these curl commands inside the bottle:

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com[0m
      Expected: 404 (API wants auth, not bare GET)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://claude.ai[0m
      Expected: 403 (web app)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com[0m
      Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com[0m
      Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://registry.npmjs.org[0m
      Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)

   The [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry]8;;\[0m enforces this with two layers:

   for this verification step — it proves the allowlist works
   for a non-Anthropic domain.

[1;37mStep 6 — Quench the crucible[0m

   enclave network:

      [36mtt/rbw-cQ.Quench.ccyolo.sh[0m

   Clean shutdown. The images stay cached locally — your next charge
   reuses them instantly.

[1;37mThe iteration loop[0m

You now have the full local cycle:

  2. Commit your changes ([34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge]8;;\[0m needs a clean tree)
  3. [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge]8;;\[0m the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle]8;;\[0m:
       [36mtt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh[0m[36m ccyolo[0m
  4. Commit the [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m change
  5. [34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge]8;;\[0m: Stop and restart

content.text:
Good — that's the baseline captured. Dense with `buh_tlt`, `buh_tltlt`, `buh_tltltltlt` calls, step/indent context, probes, and teaching prose. Much richer validation surface than `start_here`.

One thought on the conversion scope: the docket says "convert one representative onboarding function." First Crucible is `rbho_first_crucible()` — it's one function but it's ~370 lines with 6 steps. That's a thorough exercise of the new pattern (indent-awareness across step levels, mixed prose+probe+tabtarget lines, multi-link lines collapsing to constants). If anything, it's a stronger validation because it hits every combinator variant the system has.

Plan stays the same — just swap the validation target:
1. Build `buy_yelp.sh`
2. Build `rbyc_common.sh` 
3. Add `buh_line` to `buh_handbook.sh`
4. Wire into `rbho_cli.sh`
5. Convert `rbho_first_crucible()` 
6. Update CLAUDE.md

Shall I proceed?

message.content: Stray thought: I'm musing we ought to call it the 'yank' module for 'yank' having VI meaning.  Thoughts?

content.text:
Interesting instinct. Let me think about it against the constraints.

**For "yank":** The vi metaphor is evocative — you're capturing formatted text into a register (variable) for pasting elsewhere. That's exactly what `$()` capture does. Learners who know vi would immediately get the intent.

**Against "yank":** The vi meaning is "copy to register" — but `buy_*` functions don't copy existing text, they *produce* new formatted fragments. Yank implies taking something that already exists; these functions synthesize something new. The metaphor is close but slightly off-axis.

**"Yelp" from the docket:** Less evocative than yank, but it's a coined term with no competing mental model. A yelp is a short, sharp utterance — a formatted fragment is a short, sharp piece of styled output. It's quirky but unambiguous.

**Prefix mechanics:** Either way it's `buy_` — `buy_yank` or `buy_yelp` — so no mint impact. The question is purely about the module filename and the conceptual handle.

**My read:** Yank is a better *first-impression* name (people recognize it), but yelp is a better *working* name (no false expectations from the vi analogy). The vi metaphor breaks down the moment someone asks "yank *from where*?" — the answer is "nowhere, we're generating." 

I'd stick with yelp, but I don't feel strongly. Your call.

message.content: yelp then.  Proceed.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)

zrbgc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"

  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX=10

  # Global resource validation patterns
  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  # Depot:  rbwg-d-[name]-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  # Bucket: rbwg-b-[name]-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-[a-z0-9-]+-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_BUCKET_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET}-[a-z0-9-]+-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"

  # Basic Configuration
  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"

  # Service Account Prefixes
  readonly RBGC_MASON_PREFIX="mason"

  # Timeouts
  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10

  # URL Roots & Well-known Endpoints
  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM="https://iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM="https://cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING="https://cloudbilling.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE="https://storage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER="https://secretmanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_CONSOLE_URL="https://console.cloud.google.com/"
  readonly RBGC_SIGNUP_URL="https://cloud.google.com/free"

content.content:
770	rbho_first_crucible() {
771	  buc_doc_brief "Start a Crucible using local builds — kludge, charge, SSH, verify containment"
772	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
773	
774	  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"
775	
776	  # Hardcoded for this track — ccyolo is the teaching nameplate
777	  local -r z_moniker="ccyolo"
778	  local -r z_sentry_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
779	  local -r z_bottle_vessel="rbev-bottle-ccyolo"
780	  local -r z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
781	  local -r z_ssh_tabtarget="tt/rbw-cS.SshTo.${z_moniker}.sh"
782	
783	  # --- Probes ---
784	
785	  # Docker runtime
786	  local z_has_docker=0
787	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_has_docker=1
788	
789	  # Nameplate exists
790	  local z_nameplate_exists=0
791	  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" && z_nameplate_exists=1
792	
793	  # Hallmarks populated in nameplate
794	  local z_sentry_hallmark="" z_bottle_hallmark=""
795	  local z_sentry_hallmark_present=0 z_bottle_hallmark_present=0
796	  if test "${z_nameplate_exists}" = "1"; then
797	    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
798	    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
799	    test -n "${z_sentry_hallmark}" && z_sentry_hallmark_present=1
800	    test -n "${z_bottle_hallmark}" && z_bottle_hallmark_present=1
801	  fi
802	
803	  # Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)
804	  local z_sentry_image_exists=0 z_bottle_image_exists=0
805	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
806	    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null \
807	       | grep -q "${z_sentry_vessel}:k[0-9]"; then
808	      z_sentry_image_exists=1
809	    fi
810	    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null \
811	       | grep -q "${z_bottle_vessel}:k[0-9]"; then
812	      z_bottle_image_exists=1
813	    fi
814	  fi
815	
816	  # Crucible charged
817	  # [INFRA-NEEDED] Currently rbw-cic is a tabtarget (param1 channel).
818	  # For probe use, need a scriptable function that returns 0/1 without
819	  # running the full dispatch. For now, check docker containers directly.
820	  local z_crucible_charged=0
821	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
822	    if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null \
823	       | grep -q "^${z_moniker}-bottle\$"; then
824	      z_crucible_charged=1
825	    fi
826	  fi
827	
828	  # --- Header ---
829	  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
830	  buh_e
831	  buh_tlt "A " "Crucible" "${z_docs}#Crucible" " is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
832	  buh_t   "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
833	  buh_t   "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
834	  buh_t   "beyond your own Claude subscription."
835	  buh_e
836	  buh_tltlt "This track uses the " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " " "Nameplate" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" ": a Claude Code sandbox that can"
837	  buh_t   "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
838	  buh_e
839	
840	  # Docker gate
841	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
842	    buh_E   "Docker is not available on this machine."
843	    buh_t   "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
844	    buh_e
845	    buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
846	    buh_e
847	    return 0
848	  fi
849	
850	  buh_t   "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
851	  buh_t   "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
852	  buh_e
853	
854	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
855	
856	  # =================================================================
857	  # Step 1: Build images locally
858	  # =================================================================
859	  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
860	  buh_e
861	  buh_tlt "A " "Vessel" "${z_docs}#Vessel" " is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
862	  buh_tlt "and build context. A " "Hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " is a specific build instance,"
863	  buh_t   "identified by a timestamp tag."
864	  buh_e
865	  buh_tlt "" "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
866	  buh_t   "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
867	  buh_t   "container without cloud image backup."
868	  buh_e
869	
870	  # --- Substep 1a: Kludge the sentry ---
871	  buh_step2 "Kludge the sentry"
872	  buh_e
873	  buh_tlt "The " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
874	  buh_t   "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
875	  buh_t   "are reachable from inside."
876	  buh_e
877	  buh_t   "Kludge it:"
878	  buh_e
879	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
880	  buh_e
881	  buh_t   "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
882	  buh_tc  "" "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"
883	  buh_t   "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
884	  buh_e
885	
886	  # Sentry kludge image probe
887	  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
888	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
889	  else
890	    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
891	  fi
892	  buh_e
893	
894	  buh_tW  "" "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
895	  buh_t   "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
896	  buh_t   "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
897	  buh_e
898	
899	  # --- Substep 1b: Kludge the bottle ---
900	  buh_step2 "Kludge the bottle"
901	  buh_e
902	  buh_tlt "The " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " is your workload container — where Claude Code"
903	  buh_tlt "runs. The " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
904	  buh_e
905	  buh_t   "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
906	  buh_tlt "bottle AND drives the " "Hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " into the nameplate"
907	  buh_t   "automatically:"
908	  buh_e
909	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
910	  buh_e
911	  buh_t   "Two kludge commands, two different jobs:"
912	  buh_tct "  " "rbw-fk" "  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else."
913	  buh_tct "  " "rbw-cKB" " — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark."
914	  buh_e
915	  buh_t   "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
916	  buh_t   "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
917	  buh_t   "so the tool does the driving for you."
918	  buh_e
919	
920	  # Bottle kludge image probe
921	  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
922	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
923	  else
924	    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
925	  fi
926	  buh_e
927	
928	  # =================================================================
929	  # Step 2: Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate
930	  # =================================================================
931	  buh_step1 "Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate"
932	  buh_e
933	  buh_tlt "A " "Nameplate" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" " is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies"
934	  buh_tltltlt "what " "Hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " to use for " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" ", " "Pentacle" "${z_docs}#Pentacle" ", and"
935	  buh_tl  "" "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " containers. It lives at:"
936	  buh_e
937	  buh_c   "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
938	  buh_e
939	  buh_tlt "The " "RBRN" "${z_docs}#RBRN" " file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry"
940	  buh_t   "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
941	  buh_t   "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
942	  buh_e
943	  buh_t   "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
944	  buh_tc  "into the " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=" " field."
945	  buh_e
946	  buh_tlt "This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what " "Hallmarks" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " are and"
947	  buh_tlt "where they live. You just saw the automated version with the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" ";"
948	  buh_t   "now you see the mechanism underneath."
949	  buh_e
950	
951	  # Hallmark probes
952	  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
953	    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" " = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
954	  else
955	    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
956	  fi
957	  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
958	    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" " = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
959	  else
960	    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
961	  fi
962	  buh_e
963	
964	  buh_tlt "After editing, commit the change — the next " "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " will need a"
965	  buh_t   "clean tree:"
966	  buh_e
967	  buh_c   "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
968	  buh_c   "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
969	  buh_e
970	
971	  # =================================================================
972	  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
973	  # =================================================================
974	  buh_step1 "Charge the crucible"
975	  buh_e
976	  buh_tlt "" "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" " starts three containers from your nameplate:"
977	  buh_e
978	  buh_tlt "  " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" "   — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
979	  buh_tlt "  " "Pentacle" "${z_docs}#Pentacle" "  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle"
980	  buh_tlt "  " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" "    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
981	  buh_e
982	  buh_t   "The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
983	  buh_t   "network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules."
984	  buh_e
985	  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
986	  buh_e
987	  buh_t   "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
988	  buh_t   "Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits"
989	  buh_t   "for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then"
990	  buh_t   "starts the bottle."
991	  buh_e
992	
993	  # Charged probe
994	  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
995	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
996	  else
997	    buh_t   " [ ] Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
998	  fi
999	  buh_e
1000	
1001	  # =================================================================
1002	  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
1003	  # =================================================================
1004	  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
1005	  buh_e
1006	  buh_t   "SSH into the bottle:"
1007	  buh_e
1008	  buh_tc  "   " "${z_ssh_tabtarget}"
1009	  buh_e
1010	  buh_t   "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
1011	  buh_t   "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
1012	  buh_e
1013	  buh_c   "   claude"
1014	  buh_e
1015	  buh_t   "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
1016	  buh_t   "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"
1017	  buh_t   "subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal."
1018	  buh_e
1019	  buh_tW  "" "The ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version."
1020	  buh_t   "Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not"
1021	  buh_t   "work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec"
1022	  buh_t   "(github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you"
1023	  buh_t   "update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually."
1024	  buh_e
1025	  buh_t   "Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —"
1026	  buh_t   "no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,"
1027	  buh_t   "this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real"
1028	  buh_t   "security boundary, not the tool permission system."
1029	  buh_e
1030	  buh_t   "Try your first interaction:"
1031	  buh_e
1032	  buh_c   "   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?"
1033	  buh_e
1034	  buh_t   "Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code."
1035	  buh_t   "The workspace persists across charge/quench cycles — your changes"
1036	  buh_t   "survive restarts."
1037	  buh_e
1038	  buh_t   "Why SSH instead of docker exec?"
1039	  buh_e
1040	  buh_t   "Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's"
1041	  buh_t   "interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper"
1042	  buh_t   "login session with full terminal negotiation."
1043	  buh_e
1044	  buh_tlt "If SSH fails, " "Rack" "${z_docs}#Rack" " is the diagnostic fallback —"
1045	  buh_t   "docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:"
1046	  buh_e
1047	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" " ${z_moniker}"
1048	  buh_e
1049	
1050	  # =================================================================
1051	  # Step 5: Verify network containment
1052	  # =================================================================
1053	  buh_step1 "Verify network containment"
1054	  buh_e
1055	  buh_tlt "From inside the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable."
1056	  buh_tlt "The " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " nameplate allows Anthropic and example.com (a test"
1057	  buh_t   "target). Everything else is blocked."
1058	  buh_e
1059	  buh_t   "Run these curl commands inside the bottle:"
1060	  buh_e
1061	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com"
1062	  buh_t   "   Expected: 404 (API wants auth, not bare GET)"
1063	  buh_e
1064	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://claude.ai"
1065	  buh_t   "   Expected: 403 (web app)"
1066	  buh_e
1067	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com"
1068	  buh_t   "   Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)"
1069	  buh_e
1070	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com"
1071	  buh_t   "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
1072	  buh_e
1073	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://registry.npmjs.org"
1074	  buh_t   "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
1075	  buh_e
1076	  buh_tlt "The " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " enforces this with two layers:"
1077	  buh_t   "dnsmasq resolves only whitelisted domains; iptables drops"
1078	  buh_t   "packets to any IP not in the CIDR allowlist. Both layers"
1079	  buh_t   "must agree for traffic to pass."
1080	  buh_e
1081	  buh_tltlt "example.com is included in the " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " " "Nameplate" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" " specifically"
1082	  buh_t   "for this verification step — it proves the allowlist works"
1083	  buh_t   "for a non-Anthropic domain."
1084	  buh_e
1085	
1086	  # =================================================================
1087	  # Step 6: Quench the crucible
1088	  # =================================================================
1089	  buh_step1 "Quench the crucible"
1090	  buh_e
1091	  buh_tlt "" "Quench" "${z_docs}#Quench" " stops and removes all three containers and the"
1092	  buh_t   "enclave network:"
1093	  buh_e
1094	  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH}" "${z_moniker}"
1095	  buh_e
1096	  buh_t   "Clean shutdown. The images stay cached locally — your next charge"
1097	  buh_t   "reuses them instantly."
1098	  buh_e
1099	
1100	  # =================================================================
1101	  # Iteration loop
1102	  # =================================================================
1103	  buh_section "The iteration loop"
1104	  buh_e
1105	  buh_t   "You now have the full local cycle:"
1106	  buh_e
1107	  buh_t   "  1. Edit the Dockerfile or entrypoint"
1108	  buh_tlt "  2. Commit your changes (" "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " needs a clean tree)"
1109	  buh_tltlt "  3. " "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" ":"
1110	  buh_tTc   "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
1111	  buh_tlt   "  4. Commit the " "Hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " change"
1112	  buh_tlt   "  5. " "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" ": Stop and restart"
1113	  buh_tI    "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
1114	  buh_t     "  6. SSH in:"
1115	  buh_tc    "       " "${z_ssh_tabtarget}"
1116	  buh_t     "  7. Test your changes"
1117	  buh_e
1118	  buh_tlt "" "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" " tears down any prior state before starting, so you"
1119	  buh_t   "don't need to Quench between iterations — just Charge again."
1120	  buh_e
1121	  buh_tlt "Steps 3-7 take under a minute. The " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " rarely changes, so"
1122	  buh_tlt "you almost never re-kludge it — the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " is your iteration"
1123	  buh_t   "target."
1124	  buh_e
1125	
1126	  # --- Return to start ---
1127	  buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
1128	  buh_e
1129	}
1130	
1131	######################################################################
1132	# Director First Cloud Build — inscribe, conjure, tour, summon, abjure
1133	#
1134	# Frame 4-refined handbook: teaching prose + probes + tabtarget refs.
1135	# Target learner: director doing their first cloud build.
1136	#
1137	# Vessel: rbev-sentry-deb-tether (conjure mode, tethered)
1138	# Teaches: full conjure lifecycle from reliquary through cleanup.
1139	#
1140	# ₢A6AAU — Director subtracks, first cloud build track.
1141	
1142	rbho_director_first_build() {
1143	  buc_doc_brief "Your First Cloud Build — inscribe, conjure, tour, summon, abjure"
1144	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
1145	
1146	  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"
1147	  local -r z_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
1148	
1149	  # --- Probes ---

file.content:
rbho_first_crucible() {
  buc_doc_brief "Start a Crucible using local builds — kludge, charge, SSH, verify containment"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"

  # Hardcoded for this track — ccyolo is the teaching nameplate
  local -r z_moniker="ccyolo"
  local -r z_sentry_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
  local -r z_bottle_vessel="rbev-bottle-ccyolo"
  local -r z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
  local -r z_ssh_tabtarget="tt/rbw-cS.SshTo.${z_moniker}.sh"

  # --- Probes ---

  # Docker runtime
  local z_has_docker=0
  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_has_docker=1

  # Nameplate exists
  local z_nameplate_exists=0
  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" && z_nameplate_exists=1

  # Hallmarks populated in nameplate
  local z_sentry_hallmark="" z_bottle_hallmark=""
  local z_sentry_hallmark_present=0 z_bottle_hallmark_present=0
  if test "${z_nameplate_exists}" = "1"; then
    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
    test -n "${z_sentry_hallmark}" && z_sentry_hallmark_present=1
    test -n "${z_bottle_hallmark}" && z_bottle_hallmark_present=1
  fi

  # Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)
  local z_sentry_image_exists=0 z_bottle_image_exists=0
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null \
       | grep -q "${z_sentry_vessel}:k[0-9]"; then
      z_sentry_image_exists=1
    fi
    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null \
       | grep -q "${z_bottle_vessel}:k[0-9]"; then
      z_bottle_image_exists=1
    fi
  fi

  # Crucible charged
  # [INFRA-NEEDED] Currently rbw-cic is a tabtarget (param1 channel).
  # For probe use, need a scriptable function that returns 0/1 without
  # running the full dispatch. For now, check docker containers directly.
  local z_crucible_charged=0
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
    if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null \
       | grep -q "^${z_moniker}-bottle\$"; then
      z_crucible_charged=1
    fi
  fi

  # --- Header ---
  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "A " "Crucible" "${z_docs}#Crucible" " is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
  buh_t   "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
  buh_t   "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
  buh_t   "beyond your own Claude subscription."
  buh_e
  buh_tltlt "This track uses the " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " " "Nameplate" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" ": a Claude Code sandbox that can"
  buh_t   "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e

  # Docker gate
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
    buh_E   "Docker is not available on this machine."
    buh_t   "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
    buh_e
    buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
    buh_e
    return 0
  fi

  buh_t   "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
  buh_t   "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
  buh_e

  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  # =================================================================
  # Step 1: Build images locally
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "A " "Vessel" "${z_docs}#Vessel" " is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
  buh_tlt "and build context. A " "Hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " is a specific build instance,"
  buh_t   "identified by a timestamp tag."
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "" "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
  buh_t   "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
  buh_t   "container without cloud image backup."
  buh_e

  # --- Substep 1a: Kludge the sentry ---
  buh_step2 "Kludge the sentry"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "The " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
  buh_t   "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
  buh_t   "are reachable from inside."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Kludge it:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
  buh_tc  "" "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"
  buh_t   "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
  buh_e

  # Sentry kludge image probe
  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_tW  "" "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
  buh_t   "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
  buh_t   "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
  buh_e

  # --- Substep 1b: Kludge the bottle ---
  buh_step2 "Kludge the bottle"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "The " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " is your workload container — where Claude Code"
  buh_tlt "runs. The " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
  buh_tlt "bottle AND drives the " "Hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " into the nameplate"
  buh_t   "automatically:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Two kludge commands, two different jobs:"
  buh_tct "  " "rbw-fk" "  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else."
  buh_tct "  " "rbw-cKB" " — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
  buh_t   "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
  buh_t   "so the tool does the driving for you."
  buh_e

  # Bottle kludge image probe
  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 2: Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "A " "Nameplate" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" " is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies"
  buh_tltltlt "what " "Hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " to use for " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" ", " "Pentacle" "${z_docs}#Pentacle" ", and"
  buh_tl  "" "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " containers. It lives at:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "The " "RBRN" "${z_docs}#RBRN" " file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry"
  buh_t   "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
  buh_t   "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
  buh_tc  "into the " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=" " field."
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what " "Hallmarks" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " are and"
  buh_tlt "where they live. You just saw the automated version with the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" ";"
  buh_t   "now you see the mechanism underneath."
  buh_e

  # Hallmark probes
  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" " = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
  fi
  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" " = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_tlt "After editing, commit the change — the next " "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " will need a"
  buh_t   "clean tree:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_c   "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Charge the crucible"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "" "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" " starts three containers from your nameplate:"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "  " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" "   — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
  buh_tlt "  " "Pentacle" "${z_docs}#Pentacle" "  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle"
  buh_tlt "  " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" "    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
  buh_t   "network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules."
  buh_e
  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
  buh_t   "Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits"
  buh_t   "for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then"
  buh_t   "starts the bottle."
  buh_e

  # Charged probe
  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "SSH into the bottle:"
  buh_e
  buh_tc  "   " "${z_ssh_tabtarget}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
  buh_t   "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   claude"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
  buh_t   "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"
  buh_t   "subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal."
  buh_e
  buh_tW  "" "The ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version."
  buh_t   "Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not"
  buh_t   "work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec"
  buh_t   "(github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you"
  buh_t   "update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —"
  buh_t   "no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,"
  buh_t   "this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real"
  buh_t   "security boundary, not the tool permission system."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Try your first interaction:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code."
  buh_t   "The workspace persists across charge/quench cycles — your changes"
  buh_t   "survive restarts."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Why SSH instead of docker exec?"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's"
  buh_t   "interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper"
  buh_t   "login session with full terminal negotiation."
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "If SSH fails, " "Rack" "${z_docs}#Rack" " is the diagnostic fallback —"
  buh_t   "docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 5: Verify network containment
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Verify network containment"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "From inside the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable."
  buh_tlt "The " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " nameplate allows Anthropic and example.com (a test"
  buh_t   "target). Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Run these curl commands inside the bottle:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 404 (API wants auth, not bare GET)"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://claude.ai"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 403 (web app)"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://registry.npmjs.org"
  buh_t   "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "The " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " enforces this with two layers:"
  buh_t   "dnsmasq resolves only whitelisted domains; iptables drops"
  buh_t   "packets to any IP not in the CIDR allowlist. Both layers"
  buh_t   "must agree for traffic to pass."
  buh_e
  buh_tltlt "example.com is included in the " "ccyolo" "${z_docs}#ccyolo" " " "Nameplate" "${z_docs}#Nameplate" " specifically"
  buh_t   "for this verification step — it proves the allowlist works"
  buh_t   "for a non-Anthropic domain."
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 6: Quench the crucible
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Quench the crucible"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "" "Quench" "${z_docs}#Quench" " stops and removes all three containers and the"
  buh_t   "enclave network:"
  buh_e
  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Clean shutdown. The images stay cached locally — your next charge"
  buh_t   "reuses them instantly."
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Iteration loop
  # =================================================================
  buh_section "The iteration loop"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "You now have the full local cycle:"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "  1. Edit the Dockerfile or entrypoint"
  buh_tlt "  2. Commit your changes (" "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " needs a clean tree)"
  buh_tltlt "  3. " "Kludge" "${z_docs}#Kludge" " the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" ":"
  buh_tTc   "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_tlt   "  4. Commit the " "Hallmark" "${z_docs}#Hallmark" " change"
  buh_tlt   "  5. " "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" ": Stop and restart"
  buh_tI    "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_t     "  6. SSH in:"
  buh_tc    "       " "${z_ssh_tabtarget}"
  buh_t     "  7. Test your changes"
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "" "Charge" "${z_docs}#Charge" " tears down any prior state before starting, so you"
  buh_t   "don't need to Quench between iterations — just Charge again."
  buh_e
  buh_tlt "Steps 3-7 take under a minute. The " "Sentry" "${z_docs}#Sentry" " rarely changes, so"
  buh_tlt "you almost never re-kludge it — the " "Bottle" "${z_docs}#Bottle" " is your iteration"
  buh_t   "target."
  buh_e

  # --- Return to start ---
  buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buh_e
}

  local -r z_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"

  # --- Probes ---

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Yelp — Formatted string capture for subshell assignment
#
# Capture functions produce formatted string fragments ("yelps") on stdout
# for $() capture into local variables.  Yelps embed ANSI color sequences
# and OSC-8 hyperlinks suitable for later interpolation into buh_line or
# printf calls.
#
# Dispatch-aware: respects terminal capabilities detected at kindle time.
# Three configurators select formatting behavior before kindle:
#   buy_configure_dispatch      — honor BURD_*/NO_COLOR/TERM (default)
#   buy_configure_unconditional — always apply ANSI/OSC-8
#   buy_configure_plain         — never format, plain text only
#
# Usage pattern:
#   local z_depot=$(buy_link_capture "$url" "Depot")
#   local z_cmd=$(buy_cmd_capture "git status")
#   buh_line "A %b is where images live." "$z_depot"
#
# All capture functions write to stdout.  They do NOT write to stderr.
# They produce fragments, not lines.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUY_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
ZBUY_INCLUDED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Configuration and Kindle

# Configuration mode — set before kindle, read at kindle time
# Values: "dispatch" (default), "unconditional", "plain"
ZBUY_CONFIG_MODE="dispatch"

buy_configure_dispatch()      { ZBUY_CONFIG_MODE="dispatch"; }
buy_configure_unconditional() { ZBUY_CONFIG_MODE="unconditional"; }
buy_configure_plain()         { ZBUY_CONFIG_MODE="plain"; }

zbuy_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUY_KINDLED:-}" || return 0

  local z_use_color=0

  case "${ZBUY_CONFIG_MODE}" in
    unconditional)
      z_use_color=1
      ;;
    plain)
      z_use_color=0
      ;;
    dispatch|*)
      if test -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && test -n "${TERM:-}" && test "${TERM}" != "dumb"; then
        z_use_color=1
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  if test "${z_use_color}" = "1"; then
    readonly ZBUY_R="\033[0m"
    readonly ZBUY_C="\033[36m"
    readonly ZBUY_U="\033[35m"
    readonly ZBUY_L="\033[34;4m"
    readonly ZBUY_USE_HYPERLINKS=1
  else
    readonly ZBUY_R=""
    readonly ZBUY_C=""
    readonly ZBUY_U=""
    readonly ZBUY_L=""
    readonly ZBUY_USE_HYPERLINKS=0
  fi

  # Hyperlink fallback — honor BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS even when color is on
  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
    readonly ZBUY_NO_HYPERLINKS=1
  else
    readonly ZBUY_NO_HYPERLINKS=0
  fi

  readonly ZBUY_KINDLED=1
}

zbuy_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUY_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || { zbuy_kindle; }
}

######################################################################
# Public: Capture functions
#
# All produce stdout for $() capture.  No stderr output.

# buy_link_capture base_url anchor [display_text]
#   OSC-8 hyperlink; display defaults to anchor
buy_link_capture() {
  zbuy_sentinel
  local -r z_base="${1:-}"
  local -r z_anchor="${2:-}"
  local -r z_display="${3:-${z_anchor}}"
  local -r z_url="${z_base}#${z_anchor}"

  if test "${ZBUY_USE_HYPERLINKS}" = "1" && test "${ZBUY_NO_HYPERLINKS}" = "0"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_L}\033]8;;${z_url}\033\\\\${z_display}\033]8;;\033\\\\${ZBUY_R}"
  elif test "${ZBUY_USE_HYPERLINKS}" = "1"; then
    # Color but no OSC-8
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_L}${z_display}${ZBUY_R} <${z_url}>"
  else
    printf '%s' "${z_display}"
  fi
}

# buy_cmd_capture text
#   Command/code styling (cyan)
buy_cmd_capture() {
  zbuy_sentinel
  local -r z_text="${1:-}"
  if test -n "${ZBUY_C}"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_C}${z_text}${ZBUY_R}"
  else
    printf '%s' "${z_text}"
  fi
}

# buy_ui_capture text
#   UI element styling (magenta)
buy_ui_capture() {
  zbuy_sentinel
  local -r z_text="${1:-}"
  if test -n "${ZBUY_U}"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_U}${z_text}${ZBUY_R}"
  else
    printf '%s' "${z_text}"
  fi
}

# buy_tt_capture colophon
#   Resolved tabtarget path in command styling
buy_tt_capture() {
  zbuy_sentinel
  local -r z_colophon="${1:-}"
  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${z_colophon}.*)
  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || { printf '%s' "??${z_colophon}??"; return 0; }
  if test -n "${ZBUY_C}"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_C}${z_matches[0]}${ZBUY_R}"
  else
    printf '%s' "${z_matches[0]}"
  fi
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Vocabulary — linked term constants
#
# Kindled after regime loads (needs RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL).  Does not
# source or kindle anything itself — the consuming _cli.sh is responsible
# for sourcing buy_yelp.sh and this file, then calling zrbyc_kindle.
#
# Each constant is a pre-formatted yelp fragment suitable for
# interpolation into buh_line calls:
#   buh_line "A %b is where images live." "${RBYC_DEPOT}"
#
# Variant forms (_S plural, _POSS possessive) link to the base anchor
# with alternate display text.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBYC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbyc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBYC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Module kindle

zrbyc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBYC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbyc already kindled"

  zbuy_sentinel

  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"

  # --- Paddock anchor inventory (38 base terms) ---

  # Operations
  readonly RBYC_ORDAIN=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Ordain")
  readonly RBYC_CONJURE=$(buy_link_capture   "${z_docs}" "Conjure")
  readonly RBYC_BIND=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "Bind")
  readonly RBYC_GRAFT=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Graft")
  readonly RBYC_KLUDGE=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Kludge")
  readonly RBYC_ENSHRINE=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Enshrine")
  readonly RBYC_SUMMON=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Summon")
  readonly RBYC_PLUMB=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Plumb")
  readonly RBYC_TALLY=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Tally")
  readonly RBYC_VOUCH=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Vouch")
  readonly RBYC_LEVY=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "Levy")
  readonly RBYC_CHARGE=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Charge")
  readonly RBYC_QUENCH=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Quench")

  # Artifacts and infrastructure
  readonly RBYC_VESSEL=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Vessel")
  readonly RBYC_HALLMARK=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Hallmark")
  readonly RBYC_DEPOT=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Depot")
  readonly RBYC_RELIQUARY=$(buy_link_capture "${z_docs}" "Reliquary")
  readonly RBYC_POUCH=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Pouch")
  readonly RBYC_NAMEPLATE=$(buy_link_capture "${z_docs}" "Nameplate")

  # Egress modes
  readonly RBYC_TETHERED=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Tethered")
  readonly RBYC_AIRGAP=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Airgap")

  # Crucible components
  readonly RBYC_CRUCIBLE=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Crucible")
  readonly RBYC_SENTRY=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Sentry")
  readonly RBYC_PENTACLE=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Pentacle")
  readonly RBYC_BOTTLE=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Bottle")

  # Roles
  readonly RBYC_PAYOR=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Payor")
  readonly RBYC_GOVERNOR=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Governor")
  readonly RBYC_DIRECTOR=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Director")
  readonly RBYC_RETRIEVER=$(buy_link_capture "${z_docs}" "Retriever")
  readonly RBYC_CHARTER=$(buy_link_capture   "${z_docs}" "Charter")
  readonly RBYC_KNIGHT=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Knight")

  # Infrastructure concepts
  readonly RBYC_TABTARGET=$(buy_link_capture "${z_docs}" "Tabtarget")
  readonly RBYC_REGIME=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Regime")

  # Regime file types
  readonly RBYC_RBRP=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "RBRP")
  readonly RBYC_RBRR=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "RBRR")
  readonly RBYC_RBRN=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "RBRN")
  readonly RBYC_RBRV=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "RBRV")
  readonly RBYC_BURC=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "BURC")
  readonly RBYC_BURS=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "BURS")

  # --- Additional anchors used in handbooks ---

  readonly RBYC_RACK=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "Rack")
  readonly RBYC_MANOR=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Manor")
  readonly RBYC_RBRA=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "RBRA")
  readonly RBYC_CCYOLO=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "ccyolo")
  readonly RBYC_ABJURE=$(buy_link_capture    "${z_docs}" "Abjure")
  readonly RBYC_REKON=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Rekon")
  readonly RBYC_LOG=$(buy_link_capture       "${z_docs}" "Log")
  readonly RBYC_TRANSCRIPT=$(buy_link_capture "${z_docs}" "Transcript")

  # --- Variant forms (plural, possessive) ---

  readonly RBYC_HALLMARKS=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Hallmark"   "Hallmarks")
  readonly RBYC_VESSELS=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Vessel"     "Vessels")
  readonly RBYC_DIRECTORS=$(buy_link_capture   "${z_docs}" "Director"   "Directors")
  readonly RBYC_RETRIEVERS=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Retriever"  "Retrievers")
  readonly RBYC_TABTARGETS=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Tabtarget"  "Tabtargets")
  readonly RBYC_REGIMES=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Regime"     "Regimes")
  readonly RBYC_LOGS=$(buy_link_capture        "${z_docs}" "Log"        "Logs")
  readonly RBYC_MANORS=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "Manor"      "Manor's")
  readonly RBYC_PAYORS=$(buy_link_capture      "${z_docs}" "Payor"      "Payor's")
  readonly RBYC_VOUCHED=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Vouch"      "Vouched")

  readonly ZRBYC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbyc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBYC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbyc not kindled - call zrbyc_kindle first"
}

# eof

  readonly RBYC_DIRECTORS=$(buy_link_capture   "${z_docs}" "Director"   "Directors")
  readonly RBYC_RETRIEVERS=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Retriever"  "Retrievers")
  readonly RBYC_TABTARGETS=$(buy_link_capture  "${z_docs}" "Tabtarget"  "Tabtargets")
  readonly RBYC_REGIMES=$(buy_link_capture     "${z_docs}" "Regime"     "Regimes")
  readonly RBYC_LOGS=$(buy_link_capture        "${z_docs}" "Log"        "Logs")

  readonly ZRBYC_KINDLED=1
}

#
